compiled_state.title: Automatic version bumps not running since 1.0.0 release
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Commit fb58b9d8 (2026-03-30, fix(571a-3c6f): replace relative path derivation with git rev-parse in plugin scripts) accidentally overwrote the version field in plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json from 0.33.0 to 1.0.0. Since that commit, multiple epics have been merged to main without version bumps occurring — bump-version.sh reads version.file_path from dso-config.conf (which points to plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json), so all subsequent bumps would have incremented from 1.0.0 if they ran at all. Expected: version should be ~0.36.0+ reflecting the epics merged since 0.33.0. Fix: restore the version to the correct value and verify bump-version.sh is being invoked during merge-to-main.sh.

comments.body: Correction: the 1.0.0 version was intentional, not accidental. The bug is that automatic version bumps (via bump-version.sh during merge-to-main.sh) have not been running since the 1.0.0 release. Multiple epics have merged without incrementing the version past 1.0.0. Expected: version should be ~1.3.0+ reflecting epics merged since 1.0.0.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Root cause found: merge-to-main.sh only runs bump-version.sh when --bump is explicitly passed. Without it, the version_bump phase logs 'INFO: --bump not specified -- skipping version bump' and moves on. Since --bump is not passed by default during worktree merges, no automatic version bumps have been occurring. Fix options: (1) make --bump the default behavior when version.file_path is configured, or (2) have /dso:sprint pass --bump when invoking merge-to-main.sh.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Root cause already identified in prior session.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation confirmed. Fix applied in this session.
comments.author: Test
